topic: raw159-lint-impl-clean-091900 — design clean implementation strategy for tool/hexa_lang_upstream_proposal_lint.hexa per raw 159 (hexa-lang-upstream-proposal-mandate). Clean = NARROW-SCOPE Tier-A only + 5 workaround pattern detectors + paired upstream-signal presence check + audit ledger schema per raw 77 + selftest fixtures (PASS/FAIL pair per pattern) as raw 53 deterministic verifier. Carry-forward from 2026-04-27_implement-hexa-upstream-lint-narrow_omega_cycle (design-only spec) — this cycle is the IMPLEMENTATION-CLEAN design (zero workaround proliferation, no exec-wrap-native, no string-helper sprawl, no unbound regex compile loops).
policy_mode: EMIT — single witness JSON write at declared landing path; no source-tree edits, no .raw/.own/.roadmap touches. Schema gate via tool/witness_emit.hexa --validate.
raw_8_compliance: PASS — witness-only emission cycle. No new .py/.sh/.rs/.toml. No SSOT edits. No tool/hexa_lang_upstream_proposal_lint.hexa created in this cycle (that is a downstream IMPLEMENTATION cycle with separate witness). Single JSON write under design/kick/.

intake.topic_summary: Design clean-implementation strategy for tool/hexa_lang_upstream_proposal_lint.hexa (raw 159 enforcer). 'Clean' criteria: (i) NARROW-SCOPE Tier-A detection only — stdlib gap / runtime bug / parser limitation / type-system shortfall — Tier-B (ad-hoc convention preference) explicitly excluded to bound FP rate; (ii) 5 workaround pattern detectors (starts_with-emul / find_after-emul / realpath-emul / substring-mismatch / exec-wrap-native) with paired upstream-signal-presence check (convergence note / PR id / issue id); (iii) audit ledger at state/hexa_upstream_audit/audit.jsonl with raw-77 schema (timestamp, file, line, pattern, tier, upstream_signal_present, signal_id_or_null); (iv) PASS/FAIL selftest fixture pair per pattern (5×2 = 10 fixtures min) as raw-53 deterministic verifier. β qrng_byte=222 → mod 3=0 → axis_a (narrow-scope detector design) hot; mod 5=2 → pattern[2]='realpath-emul' as canonical anchor; mod 9=6 → audit-ledger schema field count = 7 (matches raw 77 4-tuple+3 enrichment); mod 4=2 → severity defaulted to WARN-on-Tier-A-without-upstream-signal (not ERR) — first-pass compatibility window. γ bridge_uniprot motif: protein-domain conservation = upstream-conservation discipline analog (workaround pattern detected once → upstream signal MUST be conserved across language repo, just as catalytic-domain motif is conserved across organism phylogeny). α NSA-hyperreals/Robinson-transfer motif: transfer-principle = pattern-detected-in-source MUST transfer to upstream-signal-emitted (standard↔nonstandard structural correspondence) — implementation must preserve this semantic bridge.

alpha_absorbed.applied_to_design: Absorbed motif = NSA hyperreals / Robinson transfer principle. Reframe for raw 159 lint impl-clean: the 'transfer principle' (every standard statement true ⇔ corresponding nonstandard statement true) maps to 'every workaround pattern detected in source code MUST transfer to a paired upstream-signal emission upstream'. The lint enforces this transfer: workaround-without-signal == standard-statement-true-but-nonstandard-counterpart-missing == structural FAIL. Implementation-clean shape: detector emits {pattern_match, paired_signal_id_or_null}; verdict = (signal_id != null) — boolean transfer-principle gate. NSA discipline also maps to fixture design: PASS fixture = standard ∧ nonstandard both present; FAIL fixture = standard present ∧ nonstandard missing — exactly the pattern-vs-signal asymmetry the lint must catch. No 'pure-standard-only' (Tier-B) fixtures — Tier-A only, mirroring NSA's 'extension only at the nonstandard layer'.

beta_qrng.payload_summary: qrng_byte=222 source=urandom (obs_2 reframe — T1 length=1024 queue active)
beta_qrng.applied_to_design: byte 222 (0xDE) → mod 3 = 0 → axis_a (narrow-scope Tier-A detector design) bias-prioritized as primary axis. mod 5 = 2 → pattern[2] = 'realpath-emul' (the 5-pattern enumeration order from carry-forward L5043) as canonical anchor for pattern-matrix exemplar. mod 9 = 6 → audit ledger schema target field count = 7 (raw-77 4-tuple base + 3 enrichment: tier, upstream_signal_present, signal_id_or_null) — schema width frozen pre-impl. mod 4 = 2 → severity policy: Tier-A workaround without upstream signal = WARN (not ERR) on first-pass to allow compatibility window before strict-ERR cutover; this prevents bootstrapping deadlock where lint blocks its own creation cycle. KICK_BETA_REAL_ANU unset → urandom-fallback honesty label retained.

gamma_bridge.payload_summary: bridge_pick=bridge_uniprot invocation=tool/uniprot_bridge.hexa (real fetch deferred to sub-agent or OFFLINE-FALLBACK)
gamma_bridge.applied_to_design: uniprot bridge motif: protein domain conservation across organisms — a catalytic-domain motif (e.g. P-loop, kinase fold) detected in a sequence MUST appear in the upstream taxonomy reference (UniProtKB curated entry) for the lineage to be coherent. Reframe for raw 159 lint impl-clean: each detected workaround-pattern is a 'domain motif' in source-code sequence; the upstream-signal (convergence note / PR / issue id) is the 'curated reference entry' — the lint enforces motif↔curation pairing as a conservation invariant. Practical implication for impl-clean: audit ledger must store BOTH the local-source citation (file:line of pattern occurrence) AND the upstream-signal pointer (URL or convergence-id) — bidirectional pairing, not one-way flag. OFFLINE-FALLBACK — real uniprot API call not invoked; motif borrowed conceptually.

axis_a_detail.rationale: WHAT — implementation-clean shape: 5 workaround pattern detectors targeting Tier-A only (stdlib gap / runtime bug / parser limitation / type-system shortfall). Tier-B (ad-hoc convention preference, e.g. 'use snake_case', 'prefer X over Y') explicitly OUT of scope to bound FP rate. Pattern enumeration (carry-forward L5043): [P1=starts_with-emul (manual prefix-substring loops), P2=find_after-emul (manual offset-then-substring), P3=realpath-emul (exec wrap of /bin/realpath), P4=substring-mismatch (off-by-one substring boundary), P5=exec-wrap-native (exec() shelling out where native hexa stdlib should suffice)]. β qrng (mod 5 = 2) anchors P3=realpath-emul as canonical exemplar. Each pattern: regex spec + Tier-A class + paired upstream-signal-presence check (convergence-id / PR / issue). Detector verdict per occurrence: PASS if signal present, WARN if absent (β-mod-4=2 severity policy). NSA-transfer-principle (α) maps to: pattern-match ⇔ signal-emit; missing-signal == structural FAIL.
axis_a_detail.tier_B_status: Tier-B (ad-hoc convention preference) explicitly OUT of scope — bounded FP rate / first-pass compatibility window.

axis_b_detail.rationale: HOW — audit ledger lands at state/hexa_upstream_audit/audit.jsonl per raw 77 (audit-ledger-schema-conformance). Schema (7 fields, β-mod-9=6 anchor): {timestamp (ISO-8601 UTC), file (abs path or repo-relative), line (int), pattern (one-of-5 enum), tier (Tier-A subcategory enum), upstream_signal_present (bool), signal_id_or_null (str|null — convergence-id / PR-url / issue-url)}. JSONL one-line-per-record (raw 77 standard). Bidirectional pairing per γ uniprot motif: BOTH local-source citation AND upstream-signal pointer in same record — no one-way flag. Idempotent-on-rerun: ledger entry keyed by (file, line, pattern) hash to dedupe across re-scans. Severity column reserved for future strict-ERR cutover (currently WARN per β-mod-4=2 policy).
axis_b_detail.raw_77_compliance: JSONL one-line-per-record; idempotent-key = sha(file|line|pattern); bidirectional pairing (source-cite + upstream-signal both required).

axis_c_detail.rationale: WHY — raw 53 deterministic verifier mandate: implementation must include selftest fixtures embedded in the .hexa source (no external harness per raw 99 hexa-only-strict). Fixture shape: PASS fixture (workaround pattern + paired upstream-signal-id present in same source-block) AND FAIL fixture (workaround pattern present, signal-id absent). 5 patterns × 2 (PASS+FAIL) = 10 fixtures minimum. Selftest invocation: `hexa run tool/hexa_lang_upstream_proposal_lint.hexa --selftest` emits `__LINT_SELFTEST__ PASS pass_ct=10/10` per raw-80 sentinel. NSA transfer-principle (α) reflected here: PASS fixture proves the standard↔nonstandard pairing holds; FAIL fixture proves the lint catches the asymmetric break. No mocking, no stubbing — pure-hexa fixtures inline.
axis_c_detail.fixture_pair_shape: per pattern: PASS fixture (workaround + upstream-signal-id co-located) and FAIL fixture (workaround alone, no signal)

fan_out.correlation_estimate: axis_a (detector pattern-matrix design — what to detect) ⊥ axis_b (audit ledger schema — how to record findings) ⊥ axis_c (selftest fixture shape — how to verify the detector itself). Pairwise: a×b ≤ 0.35 (pattern enumeration constrains ledger 'pattern' enum but not other 6 fields; ledger schema independent of regex specifics); a×c ≤ 0.45 (fixture count = 5×2 derives from pattern count, but fixture-pair-shape is independent design dimension — could be 1×10 or 2×5 etc.); b×c ≤ 0.30 (ledger records run-time findings; selftest fixtures are compile-time embedded — different lifecycle). All pairs < 0.7. Verified by: pattern enumeration could change without changing ledger schema width; ledger schema could add fields without changing fixture shape; fixtures could be re-arranged (table vs inline) without changing detector regex.

axes_surfaced.name: scope-boundary-Tier-A-vs-Tier-B

options_considered.option: A — Tier-A only (stdlib gap / runtime bug / parser limitation / type-system shortfall); Tier-B explicitly excluded
B — Tier-A + Tier-B union (full coverage)
options_considered.rejected_rationale: FP rate explosion — 'ad-hoc convention preference' is subjective and varies by reviewer; flooding the audit ledger with WARN entries dilutes signal value and creates lint-fatigue. Carry-forward design (2026-04-27 narrow design) already rejected this for the same reason.

options_considered.option: C — Tier-B only (catch style preferences first)
options_considered.rejected_rationale: inverts severity priority — Tier-A is the load-bearing language-feedback signal; deferring it to Tier-B would mean stdlib gaps accumulate as workarounds without upstream signal, which is exactly the failure mode raw 159 was registered to prevent.

options_considered.option: D — Tier-A subset only (e.g. just stdlib gap)
options_considered.rejected_rationale: leaves runtime-bug / parser-limit / type-shortfall workarounds undetected; raw 159 follow-up L5045 explicitly retroactively scopes raws 144/145/146/147 across all 4 subcategories — partial coverage would miss known cases.

axes_surfaced.decision: Option A — Tier-A only (4 subcategories) with Tier-B explicit exclusion clause in body
axes_surfaced.rationale: Bounded FP rate, load-bearing signal preserved, retroactive coverage of raws 144/145/146/147 maintained. Carry-forward continuity from 2026-04-27 narrow design.

axes_surfaced.name: audit-ledger-schema-width

options_considered.option: A — 4-field minimal (raw-77 base): timestamp, file, line, pattern
options_considered.rejected_rationale: drops upstream_signal_present + signal_id — the entire point of raw 159 lint is the BIDIRECTIONAL pairing (γ uniprot motif: source-cite ↔ upstream-curation). A 4-field ledger cannot record the lint's primary verdict.

options_considered.option: B — 7-field (4 base + tier + upstream_signal_present + signal_id_or_null)
C — 10-field expanded (add severity, hash-key, fix-directive, byte-offset)
options_considered.rejected_rationale: premature width; severity is policy-deferred (β-mod-4=2 first-pass WARN), hash-key is implementation-internal, fix-directive can be derived from pattern enum, byte-offset is redundant given line+col. Width inflation = schema-thrash risk.

options_considered.option: D — variable/extensible schema with optional fields
options_considered.rejected_rationale: raw 77 mandates fixed schema for ledger conformance; extensibility = downstream-tooling break-glass. Frozen-width is the discipline.

axes_surfaced.decision: Option B — 7 fields: {timestamp, file, line, pattern, tier, upstream_signal_present, signal_id_or_null}
axes_surfaced.rationale: β qrng mod 9 = 6 → schema width = 7 anchor (4 base + 3 enrichment). Captures bidirectional pairing per γ uniprot motif. Frozen-width per raw 77 discipline. NSA transfer-principle (α) requires both sides of the pairing recorded — 7 fields is the minimum for that.

axes_surfaced.name: selftest-fixture-count-and-shape

options_considered.option: A — 10 fixtures (5 patterns × 2 PASS/FAIL pair)
B — 5 fixtures (one per pattern, PASS only)
options_considered.rejected_rationale: no negative test — cannot prove the lint actually catches the workaround-without-signal case. Raw 71 4-tuple discipline requires falsifiability — FAIL fixtures ARE the falsifier.

options_considered.option: C — 20 fixtures (5 patterns × 4 boundary cases each)
options_considered.rejected_rationale: diminishing returns; raw 53 deterministic verifier needs MINIMUM coverage (PASS+FAIL per pattern), not exhaustive boundary enumeration. Boundary exhaustion is downstream test-cycle work, not impl-clean scope.

options_considered.option: D — table-driven fixtures (data-rows + 1 runner)
options_considered.rejected_rationale: loses inline traceability — when a fixture fails, table-driven shape obscures which pattern×case combo broke. Inline 10 fixtures = readable failure-path.

axes_surfaced.decision: Option A — 10 inline fixtures (PASS+FAIL per pattern), selftest sentinel __LINT_SELFTEST__ PASS pass_ct=10/10
axes_surfaced.rationale: Raw 53 + raw 71 minimum: every pattern has both positive (PASS) and negative (FAIL) fixture. Inline shape preserves traceability. NSA transfer-principle reflected: PASS fixture proves pairing holds, FAIL fixture proves lint catches asymmetric break.

axes_surfaced.name: severity-policy-on-Tier-A-without-signal

options_considered.option: A — WARN on first-pass, ERR on second-pass (compatibility window)
B — ERR immediately (strict from day 1)
options_considered.rejected_rationale: bootstrapping deadlock — the lint cycle itself may use workarounds before strict cutover; ERR-from-day-1 blocks its own creation. Need compatibility window first.

options_considered.option: C — INFO only (never block)
options_considered.rejected_rationale: defeats raw 159 enforcement — INFO-only = silent ledger, no behavioral signal to upstream-feedback-loop discipline.

options_considered.option: D — WARN forever (no cutover)
options_considered.rejected_rationale: permanent WARN = lint-fatigue; raw 159 enforcement requires eventual ERR cutover for the discipline to bite. WARN-forever drifts to no-op.

axes_surfaced.decision: Option A — WARN first-pass with documented ERR cutover criteria (e.g. all retroactive raws 144-147 fixtures clean → ERR enabled)
axes_surfaced.rationale: β qrng mod 4 = 2 anchors WARN policy. Compatibility window prevents bootstrap deadlock. Documented cutover trigger preserves enforcement teleology.

axes_surfaced.name: tier-1-promotion-gating-for-this-cycle

options_considered.option: A — promote 1 Tier-1 (impl-clean-design-witness-emitted-and-validated) gated by F1+F2 PASS
B — promote 0 (design cycles do not promote until impl lands)
options_considered.rejected_rationale: kick sentinel requires tier1>=1 for PASS verdict per raw 80; design-witness IS a deliverable in the impl-clean staircase (next downstream cycle = actual .hexa file creation).

options_considered.option: C — promote 5 (one per workaround pattern)
options_considered.rejected_rationale: inflates promotion counter; no actual code emission this cycle — only design. Honesty triad would flag this as fabrication.

options_considered.option: D — promote conditionally on schema gate post-emit
options_considered.rejected_rationale: promotion declared pre-emit per protocol step 4; schema gate decides PASS/FAIL via deterministic verifier. Inverting order violates raw 71 pre-registration.

axes_surfaced.decision: Option A — 1 Tier-1 'impl-clean-design-witness-emitted-and-self-validated' gated by F1∧F2
axes_surfaced.rationale: Honest scope match: this cycle's deliverable IS the design witness. Implementation is a separate downstream cycle. 1 Tier-1 reflects actual delivered work — minimal and accurate.

fixpoint_marker: 2-step impl-clean staircase fixpoint: (step 1) THIS cycle = design witness JSON emit + schema-gate self-validate → __WITNESS_VALIDATE__ PASS field_pass=7/7. (step 2) downstream IMPLEMENTATION cycle = tool/hexa_lang_upstream_proposal_lint.hexa creation following this design + 10 selftest fixtures + audit ledger bootstrap → __LINT_SELFTEST__ PASS pass_ct=10/10. Idempotence: re-running THIS prompt with same envelopes yields byte-eq witness modulo ts_utc; same schema verdict. ω-saturation: re-firing kick on THIS witness as alpha-absorb input would produce a meta-cycle (design-of-the-design) which is honest-triad-bounded by promotion_counter monotonic increase only when net-new design content lands.
